...........According to the latest UN report, sea levels could rise by a metre over the next ten years. The report warns that many serious impacts of climate change are already inevitable, whether emissions are controlled or not. Warming over the last twenty years has already made the oceans hotter and more acidic. Also, melting glaciers and ice sheets are causing sea levels to rise at worrying rates, scientists say. Hundreds of millions of people worldwide who live in low-lying coastal areas, from villages to megacities, will experience more intense tropical cyclones and storms, extreme winds and flooding.
UN warns bad weather is on the way
Glaciers currently facing serious meltdown
Future problems for certain coastal communities
Complete the text
 Choose the correct words to fill the gap.
Select the correct answer from the three options. And gave my only answer .

The correct words to fill the gap are:

Future problems for certain coastal communities

So the complete text would be:

Future problems for certain coastal communities. According to the latest UN report, sea levels could rise by a metre over the next ten years. The report warns that many serious impacts of climate change are already inevitable, whether emissions are controlled or not. Warming over the last twenty years has already made the oceans hotter and more acidic. Also, melting glaciers and ice sheets are causing sea levels to rise at worrying rates, scientists say. Hundreds of millions of people worldwide who live in low-lying coastal areas, from villages to megacities, will experience more intense tropical cyclones and storms, extreme winds and flooding.

..........While saving money and coming up with a solid business plan are important steps when launching a new business, identifying the right name for it can be just as crucial – and it is a step which is often overlooked by inexperienced businesspeople. Your company’s name is the first thing most customers will hear, so it is important that it is memorable, smart and unique. Try to avoid names that sound too much like other companies, and check that the name you’ve chosen does not mean something rude! Once you’ve done that, the next step is to find the perfect logo.
Why copying other businesses is a smart approach
A company name that matches its logo
First impressions are important in business
Complete the text
 Choose the correct words to fill the gap.
Select the correct answer from the three options. And gave my only answer .

The correct words to fill the gap are:

First impressions are important in business

So the complete text would be:

First impressions are important in business. While saving money and coming up with a solid business plan are important steps when launching a new business, identifying the right name for it can be just as crucial – and it is a step which is often overlooked by inexperienced businesspeople. Your company’s name is the first thing most customers will hear, so it is important that it is memorable, smart and unique. Try to avoid names that sound too much like other companies, and check that the name you’ve chosen does not mean something rude! Once you’ve done that, the next step is to find the perfect logo.

........It’s not often that you get something for nothing, but that’s now the case with the town centre car park. When the council recently announced a planned rise in the fee for using the car park, town residents had had enough. In an article published in the local newspaper, residents said they would stay away from the town centre and shop in the big out-of-town retail park where parking is free. In a surprising statement, the council have said that from the first of next month, fees for the town centre car park will be abolished.
Victory for the local council
Power to the people
Large retail parks always win
Complete the text
 Choose the correct words to fill the gap.
Select the correct answer from the three options. And gave my only answer .

The correct words to fill the gap are:

Power to the people

So the complete text would be:

Power to the people. It’s not often that you get something for nothing, but that’s now the case with the town centre car park. When the council recently announced a planned rise in the fee for using the car park, town residents had had enough. In an article published in the local newspaper, residents said they would stay away from the town centre and shop in the big out-of-town retail park where parking is free. In a surprising statement, the council have said that from the first of next month, fees for the town centre car park will be abolished.

......The newly released Laptop 4 is the most exciting computer released in years. Although the design remains the same as the previous model, something which I'd prefer to have been updated, there are many new features which make it worth the expensive price tag. I found it more than delivers on battery life, with the battery lasting up to 15 hours without having to recharge – far longer than any of its competitors. As well as the new and improved speakers which provide a clearer sound than the Laptop 3, the Laptop 4 includes a unique chip which, during testing, provided super-fast performance and a bright and colourful display.
An advert for a new product
A review of a new product
An announcement of a new product
Complete the text
 Choose the correct words to fill the gap.
Select the correct answer from the three options. And gave my only answer .

The correct words to fill the gap are:

A review of a new product

So the complete text would be:

A review of a new product. The newly released Laptop 4 is the most exciting computer released in years. Although the design remains the same as the previous model, something which I'd prefer to have been updated, there are many new features which make it worth the expensive price tag. I found it more than delivers on battery life, with the battery lasting up to 15 hours without having to recharge – far longer than any of its competitors. As well as the new and improved speakers which provide a clearer sound than the Laptop 3, the Laptop 4 includes a unique chip which, during testing, provided super-fast performance and a bright and colourful display.

.........The crisis at Inside Tech continues today as employees are affected by a never-before-seen virus. The virus appears to have exploited a weak point in the company's protection software. Once installed, it creates its own system of complex barriers, and even top IT specialists can't get past them. A fast and easy solution looks unlikely. This case is a strong warning to companies to ensure their systems have up-to-date protection software. It has also caused general concern in the IT community about the sophisticated nature of new viruses and the consequences this may have.
New technological threats require attention
Complicated network problems linked to employee mistake
Heavy job losses for IT department
Complete the text
 Choose the correct words to fill the gap.
Select the correct answer from the three options. And gave my only answer .

The correct words to fill the gap are:

New technological threats require attention

So the complete text would be:

New technological threats require attention. The crisis at Inside Tech continues today as employees are affected by a never-before-seen virus. The virus appears to have exploited a weak point in the company's protection software. Once installed, it creates its own system of complex barriers, and even top IT specialists can't get past them. A fast and easy solution looks unlikely. This case is a strong warning to companies to ensure their systems have up-to-date protection software. It has also caused general concern in the IT community about the sophisticated nature of new viruses and the consequences this may have.

.......It has become widely accepted in some developed countries that children have been affected more by stress and conditions like depression in recent years. This has now led to some schools providing "mindfulness" lessons during school time. Mindfulness training teaches children to pay attention to their own feelings and current experience without judging and is said to make people calmer and less likely to be depressed. It also builds confidence and self-esteem and seems to help with exam nerves. Negative behaviour by children in school is also reduced. While it is not a miracle cure, schools report it is very worthwhile.
Improving the mental health of children
Helping teachers cope with stress
How to deal with bad behaviour
Complete the text
 Choose the correct words to fill the gap.
Select the correct answer from the three options. And gave my only answer .

The correct words to fill the gap are:

Improving the mental health of children

So the complete text would be:

Improving the mental health of children. It has become widely accepted in some developed countries that children have been affected more by stress and conditions like depression in recent years. This has now led to some schools providing "mindfulness" lessons during school time. Mindfulness training teaches children to pay attention to their own feelings and current experience without judging and is said to make people calmer and less likely to be depressed. It also builds confidence and self-esteem and seems to help with exam nerves. Negative behaviour by children in school is also reduced. While it is not a miracle cure, schools report it is very worthwhile.

Group interviews are an interview technique where multiple candidates are interviewed together at the same time. Group interviews have increased in popularity in recent decades. They are frequently described as a “red flag” by potential employees, and very few candidates are fans of this approach, but there are certain merits to them.
The main reason companies opt for group interviews is to save time and reduce costs. This is often the case for companies that need to hire a lot of people at the same time, e.g., start-ups, whose budgets may be stretched thin already. Another popular reason behind group interviews is that they supposedly offer an opportunity to observe candidates in action. This is critical especially for jobs that require strong people skills.
Why are group interviews so disliked by candidates, then? Firstly, they’re impersonal and offer candidates fewer chances to shine. They can also last for many hours, which many candidates treat as a sign of a company that cares little for their time. Finally, group interviews are not appropriate for all positions or personality types, and candidates who are perfect for a role but don’t respond well to a group interview environment are at a disadvantage. What this means is that before a hiring manager opts for a group interview style, there are important questions to ask themselves: Is it the right technique for this role? Does the person who will be carrying out the group interview have the necessary skills? Finally, is this decision based on convenience, or on reasons linked to the role itself?
What does the author say about start-up companies?
Start-ups more often look for employees who are good at communicating with others
Start-ups often have less money to spend on hiring new employees
Start-ups hire more people than other companies that do group interviews
Read the long text. Read the question and select the correct answer from the three options. And gave my only answer .

The author says that start-ups often have less money to spend on hiring new employees.

So the correct answer from the options provided is:

Start-ups often have less money to spend on hiring new employees
